title: AUTOMATED BRAIN PARCELLATION IN THE PREOPERATIVE WORKUP OF ADULT-TYPE DIFFUSE GLIOMAS: WHERE ANATOMY MEETS IMAGE ANALYSIS
author: N.M. Djuraeva, Kh.V. Abdukhalimova
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Automated brain parcellation has matured from a research utility used in cohort neuroimaging into a methodologically attractive option for preoperative planning in neuro-oncology. Unlike most advanced sequences in the multiparametric MRI workup of adult-type diffuse gliomas, parcellation runs on the standard 3D T1-weighted volume already acquired for surgical navigation, requires no additional contrast injection or scan time, and outputs a quantitative map of cortical parcels and subcortical structures involved by the tumor. This review covers the conceptual rationale for incorporating parcellation into the preoperative protocol, summarizes the three pipeline families that dominate current practice (FreeSurfer, FastSurfer/FastSurferVINN, and SAMSEG), discusses the methodological caveats that arise when these tools are applied to brains distorted by mass effect and edema, and outlines the integration of parcellation outputs with diffusion tractography, perfusion metrics, and radiogenomic models. The clinical bottleneck is no longer software availability but validation on glioma cohorts, where standard atlases were not built. The review draws on 18 sources, mostly from 2019–2026.
